title: Print Last Character

content.problemStatement: Rectify the errors in the program to correctly print the last character of the string.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains a single string S (may contain spaces).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the last character of the given string on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The last characters are 'f' from "CodeChef", 'd' from "HelloWorld", and 'a' from "a".
For "OpenAI" the last character is 'I'; for "ChatGPT" it is 'T'.
A single-character string returns that character itself.

hidden.label: stress case 2
hidden.input:
10
a
bb
ccc
dddd
eeeee
ffffff
ggggggg
hhhhhhhh
iiiiiiiii
jjjjjjjjjj
hidden.expectedOutput:
a
b
c
d
e
f
g
h
i
j

hidden.label: stress case 4
hidden.input:
4
   leading spaces
trailing spaces   
  both  
normal

solution.approach: The task is straightforward: for each input string we need its last character. After reading T, we read each line as a whole string (including possible spaces). If the string is non‑empty, the character at index length‑1 is the answer. This is O(|S|) per test case for reading, and O(1) for extracting the character. The total complexity is linear in the total input size. Memory usage is O(1) besides the input buffer.
solution.timeComplexity: O(total length of all strings) overall, O(|S|) per test case.

tcGeneratorPython:
def generate_case(i, RINT, POWER):
    # Example generator for the hidden testcases defined above.
    cases = {
        1: lambda: print('1') or print('a'),
        2: lambda: print('1') or print('Z'),
        3: lambda: print('1') or print('0'),
        4: lambda: print('1') or print('!'),
        5: lambda: print('1') or print('a'*15),
        6: lambda: print('1') or print('1234567890ABCDE'),
        7: lambda: print('1') or print('Hello World!!!'),
        8: lambda: print('1') or print('!@#$%^&*()_+{}|'),
        9: lambda: print('3') or [print('test') for _ in range(3)],
        10: lambda: print('2') or [print('aaaaa') for _ in range(2)],
        11: lambda: print('3') or print('a') or print('ab') or print('abc'),
        12: lambda: print('3') or print('cba') or print('ba') or print('a'),
        13: lambda: print('1') or print('end '),
        14: lambda: print('1') or print('123456789012345'),
        15: lambda: print('1') or print('~'),
        16: lambda: print('1') or print('x'*100),
        17: lambda: print('5') or [print(s) for s in ['Hello','World','Code','Chef','!']],
        18: lambda: print('10') or [print('a'*i) for i in range(1,11)],
        19: lambda: print('20') or [print(str(i)) for i in range(1,21)],
        20: lambda: print('4') or print('   leading spaces') or print('trailing spaces   ') or print('  both  ') or print('normal')
    }
    if i in cases:
        cases[i]()
    else:
        pass
